introSection: Tronador was a Interpark roller coaster located at Parque de la Costa Tigre, Buenos Aires, Argentina.

sections.title: Ride
sections.content:
When the coaster starts, the train is slowly pulled out of the station and engages with the chain lift hill. As it ascends, riders have a clear view of the surrounding boardwalk and ocean. At the top of the lift, the train is released into a steep initial drop that immediately builds speed.

The train then enters a single vertical loop, inverting the riders completely before exiting into a straight section of track. After the loop, the train navigates a series of gentle turns and small hills that create moments of mild airtime. Finally, it approaches the brake run, gradually slowing before returning to the station. This compact looping coaster design delivers a short but classic thrill experience with a signature inversion and smooth pacing throughout the ride.

sections.title: Train
sections.content: 2 trains with 2 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 8 riders per train.

fullText:
Tronador was a Interpark roller coaster located at Parque de la Costa Tigre, Buenos Aires, Argentina.



When the coaster starts, the train is slowly pulled out of the station and engages with the chain lift hill. As it ascends, riders have a clear view of the surrounding boardwalk and ocean. At the top of the lift, the train is released into a steep initial drop that immediately builds speed.

The train then enters a single vertical loop, inverting the riders completely before exiting into a straight section of track. After the loop, the train navigates a series of gentle turns and small hills that create moments of mild airtime. Finally, it approaches the brake run, gradually slowing before returning to the station. This compact looping coaster design delivers a short but classic thrill experience with a signature inversion and smooth pacing throughout the ride.

2 trains with 2 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 8 riders per train.
